TextENGNew: ``W-what... are you a-a-all doing here...@k@vS18/04/160400017. together... w-what on earth............?!``
At the sound of that trembling voice, I slowly pried my eyelids open.
``Satoko...``
The meaningless word I uttered became a hollow sound, drifting into the darkness between the trees.
Satoko stared at us, her enormous eyes brimming with tears, her knees shaking uncontrollably.
changeReason: Old says 'arms around her knees' but Japanese 膝をがくがくと震わせ means 'knees trembling.'

TextENGNew: Beyond her line of sight lay the grotesque, blackened remains of her uncle.
The thrill of having successfully brought down our prey went ice cold in an instant.
...Satoko, why did you come here? Why did you have to come...?!
Standing before Satoko with her eyes wide in horror, something like anger surged inside me.
...Everything we did was for you, Satoko. ...It was all for your sake.
That was an undeniable fact, but it hardly seemed like the right thing to say at this moment.
I hadn't wanted her to see this. ...More than anyone else in the world... you, Satoko, were the last person who should have seen this.
So why -- why did you have to come waltzing in here?!
``...Satoko-chan, weren't you supposed to be at the festival? ...Weren't you?``
Rena's voice came out flat and toneless, almost like a threat.
Shion watched Satoko with sharp eyes, the stun gun still in her hand.
``Satoko...@k@vS18/01/160100478. What are you doing in a place like this...?``
I forced the question out.
Caught in a situation that left zero room for excuses, there was only one course of action we could take toward Satoko -- defense.
By asking questions, we could avoid giving unnecessary explanations and deny Satoko any opportunity to question us.
Get out of here, Satoko. Now.
We still have so much left to do.
Above all, we need to bury this thing right away...!
``Satoko...?``
I stepped closer, pressing her to respond.
Satoko shrank back a step, her eyes full of terror.
``You got lost, didn't you?@k@vS18/06/160600347. Satoko, let's go back to the festival together, okay?``
Shion extended a hand toward Satoko.
But Satoko pulled her own hands close to her body and took another step back.
``N-no...@k@vS18/04/160400019. That's not it...@k@vS18/04/160400020. I-I... was looking for my u-uncle...``
``Your uncle...?@k@vS18/02/160200329. I'm sure he's already back at home.``
Despite the gentle tone of her voice, the look in Rena's eyes was growing ever sharper.
...Just go, already.
That was what Rena's eyes were saying.
``U-uh, um... but...@k@vS18/04/160400022. My uncle wasn't at the house... and I d-don't know where he went...``
Her voice trembled, but she spoke with everything she had.
The air around Satoko was thick with hostility.
Satoko herself must have understood perfectly well that she was not welcome here.
The harder she tried, the more pitiful she looked in my eyes.
Nobody wanted to hear why Satoko had come here...
``I left my uncle all alone...``
``And I thought... he'd be angry at me again...``
``I-I... w-w-wasn't... expecting... th-......``
``GYAAAAAAAAAA!``
...Everything Satoko had struggled to explain was swallowed up by that final scream.
She flailed her arms wildly, as if in delirium.
Hurled to the absolute limit of shock and terror, her emotions snapped -- a muscle near her temple twitched spasmodically as she--
``D-d-don't come closer!@k@vS18/04/160400028. D-don't come over here...!@k@vS18/04/160400029. Mur... m-m-murderer! Murderer! Murderer!``
Satoko repeated the word over and over, like a broken toy.
She clung to it as though it were an exorcism chant, reciting it without end.
Murderer... murderer... murderer... murderer... murderer... murderer...
Satoko's voice burrowed into my ears.
...Stop it! Satoko, stop!
Satoko kept her hollow gaze darting between me, Rena, and Shion as she backed away, step by faltering step.
Her legs unsteady with fear.
``Hey, Satoko.@k@vS18/01/160100481. Are you all right?!``
I rushed to Satoko and grabbed her arm.
``Let go, let go let go let go let goooo!@k@vS18/04/160400032. Nooooooooooo!!``
Satoko wrenched free of my grip and shoved me in the chest. I stumbled but kept reaching for her hand.
Her small hands thrashed and flailed right in front of me, but I couldn't get a hold on them.
...Satoko! No! You've got it wrong!
...I can't explain right now, but the time will come when I can tell you everything!
...This was all to make you happy! To take back our 'rightful future' -- the irreplaceable, ordinary happiness you deserve!
``Stay away!@k@vS18/04/160400034. Nooo! Don't touch me!@k@vS18/04/160400035. Stop! Murderer!``
Satoko swung her arms, ran, staggered, tripped, rolled on the ground, scooped up handfuls of fallen leaves and hurled them in every direction.
The leaves drifted down weakly, and one brushed against my shin with a dry rustle.
changeReason: Old says 'She slowly fell into leaves' but Japanese describes falling leaves brushing against narrator's shin -- wrong subject entirely.

TextENGNew: ``Nooooooooo!@k@vS18/04/160400037. S-somebodyyyy!@k@vS18/04/160400038. There are m-murderers! There are murderers here!``
Satoko's piercing shrieks split the air.
Screams of terror and despair echoed around us, again and again.
The sound of her voice grated on me.
I was growing more and more irritated.
Scream all you want, but if you do that, everything we accomplished will be for nothing...!
Just as the impulse to strike Satoko seized me--
Satoko, who had been screaming with all her might, suddenly crumpled to the ground.
The screaming stopped. And the forest reclaimed its silence.
Shion had pressed the stun gun to the back of Satoko's neck.
``...Let's put her to sleep for a while.``
Shion's voice sounded colder than I had ever heard it.
Her vacant eyes looked down at Satoko.
I wasn't the only one who had been feeling disturbed...
Satoko's pitiful screams still echoed in my ears.
I pitied Satoko from the bottom of my heart.
But at the same time, the ruthless look in Shion's eyes disturbed me even more.
...Were we, perhaps, doing something truly monstrous here?
A doubt I should never have entertained reared its head.
changeReason: New captures 思ってはいけない (shouldn't think) better than old's neutral 'doubts were beginning to rise.'

TextENGNew: Shion, are you leading us down a path from which there's no return...?
Shion... when you killed Teppei and shocked Satoko unconscious, did you really do it for Satoko's sake...?
I was beginning to look at Shion's cold, dark eyes with something approaching revulsion.
...Shion had brought the stun gun -- something that was never part of the plan.
I could have handled the killing alone. ...The reason I'd insisted on that was because I didn't want Shion and Rena to become killers.
...It was for everyone's sake.
And yet.
Shion had ignored my wishes and brought her own weapon.
What the hell was she thinking...?
...Did she have other plans I didn't know about?
Having an accomplice whose true nature I couldn't fathom was deeply unsettling.
``We lost a lot of time, so we need to hurry and finish up.@k@vS18/02/160200331. Let's take care of things as planned.``
Rena's composed voice snapped me back to my senses.
``We need to carry him to the hole.``
I nodded in silence and grabbed Teppei by the head. Rena took one foot, Shion the other.
We carried Teppei. ...No one said a word.
Hey, what are you all thinking?
I stole a glance at Rena's profile. In the darkness, only her glistening sweat caught the faint light.
We lost a lot of time...?
I turned Rena's earlier words over in my mind.
Why had she put it that way? Sure, Satoko's appearance had cost us time. But...

The fact that someone had witnessed us committing murder was a catastrophe of the highest order. I couldn't understand how Rena could so casually sum it up as 'losing time,' as if it were a minor scheduling issue.
If her mind and her face were telling entirely different stories... then what did that blank expression she wore right now actually mean...?
Looking at Shion, her hair was soaked through, plastered to her neck by the sweat.
Whether neither of them noticed my gaze, or simply pretended not to, they didn't glance my way even once.
...Hey, everyone. What are you all thinking?
Why am I not feeling the slightest bit happy?
Why am I weighed down by such heavy feelings?
...I should have been feeling light and liberated.
Because we did a good thing, didn't we? We did the right thing. With everything we had...!
We're comrades, at least, aren't we...? Comrades who buried evil together...
And yet, for some reason... the loneliness terrified me beyond words.
changeReason: Old says 'why am I so afraid of being alone?'; new says 'the loneliness terrified me beyond words.' Old frames as a question, new as a statement. JPN uses question form, but intensity differs

TextENGNew: What was this...?! Fear?!
Don't make me laugh. What was there to be afraid of?
Getting cold feet now? That was absurd. There was no reason for it.
Because I did something good, didn't I...?
Hey, everyone. That's right... isn't it?
Tell me it is. We built a monument to justice together... didn't we?
``Right here, right?``
...*Thud.* Teppei's body dropped onto the fallen leaves.
Without a word, Rena nudged Teppei with the toe of her shoe and rolled him into the hole.
Without a word, Shion tossed what appeared to be a torn scrap of clothing in after him.
``Are you tired, Keiichi-kun?@k@vS18/02/160200334. ...We need to do this together, okay?``
Rena leaned in to look at my face. But there was no concern in her eyes.
I didn't dare think about why she insisted we do it together.
I took a deep breath to calm myself, pulling air all the way down to the bottom of my gut.
Cool oxygen flowed into my body...
I repeated it again and again, trying to reclaim some composure.
...This was no time to be tired, and overthinking would get me nowhere. I just needed to do what needed to be done.
Not a single one of us could escape this bond of complicity. That much was obvious.
Through the toes of my sneakers, I felt something sickeningly soft. I, too, had kicked something into the hole.
What was it...? It didn't matter. ...I didn't want to know.
I didn't want to think about what I was doing right now.
I was performing a task. ...Physical labor that would surely serve mankind someday, an investment in a noble future, something like that.
I was surely doing something meaningful. ...Right here. Right now. ............Surely.
``...All right then, let's split up and handle our parts.``
``Yeah.``
Mustering every last scrap of false energy, I picked up the shovel once more.
``I'll handle the burial.``
``Yeah.@k@vS18/02/160200337. ...Rena will go dispose of the tools in the swamp.``
``Then I'll sink his motorcycle in the swamp.``
Rena gathered the remaining shovels and walked without hesitation toward Onigafuchi.
Shion straddled the motorcycle and took off with practiced ease.
Both their figures receded into the distance as if it were the most natural thing in the world.
Neither Rena nor Shion showed any agitation, excitement, or even grief.
They simply went about carrying out the tasks we had assigned in advance, matter-of-factly.
Their composure struck me as chillingly ruthless, and a faint dread crept in.
...That said, I couldn't afford to stand here seeing them off forever.
I had my own work to do.
I began shoveling soil onto the corpse in the hole.
Fortunately, the dried blood obscured most of Teppei's face -- beaten to a pulp like a split pomegranate.
Dirt poured mercilessly into Teppei's gaping mouth, into the ruin of his face.
Pile on the dirt, stamp it down. Pile on more, stamp it down...
Filling the hole should have been far easier than digging it. All I had to do was keep piling on the soft earth...
...Don't think about anything, Keiichi Maebara.
Just focus on what's in front of you. What's right in front of you...
My arms were getting painfully heavy.
A bone-deep exhaustion crawled through my arms and lower back, threatening to buckle my knees.
Sweat streamed down my forehead and trickled into my mouth, mixing with the dirt that had stuck to my face when I wiped it. *Grit.* The taste of earth filled my mouth.
For one fleeting moment, I wondered if I was the one being buried.
Teppei lay in that dark, cramped, filthy, bug-ridden hole, dirt being heaped onto his face, his body.
...And I -- buried alive in the dark hole of sin, having the bitter dirt of conscience shoveled over me for the rest of my life.
...Give me a break! Why should I be tormented by guilt?
Teppei is the villain here. A man with not the slightest value to the living world.
If anything, I did a good thing!
Haah... haah... haah...
I could hear my own breathing. Right -- it's quiet here. ...Because there's no one else around.
Once I finish burying him, it will all be over...
Once the corpse is buried...
*Pat pat*... *pat pat*... *pat pat*...
A warm breeze blew past, and I stopped. The trees stirred -- but it wasn't just the wind.
Someone was behind me...!
Since when...?! Who...?! For what purpose...?!
*Pat pat*... *pat pat*... *pat pat*...
I was afraid to turn around.
I had a premonition that the instant I did, everything would be over.
All the suffering, all the effort -- washed away in an instant...
``...Keiichi-kun, are you finished?``
When I turned, it was Rena and Shion. They'd apparently come back at some point.
``W-what...?@k@vS18/01/160100487. Don't scare me like that...``
``...Oh?@k@vS18/02/160200340. Did we startle you? Startle you?``
Rena looked utterly spent as well. She was making a joke, but neither of us could muster a laugh.
``Kei-chan, I'll take Satoko on my scooter.@k@vS18/06/160600352. It's dangerous to leave her lying over there.@k@vS18/06/160600353. I'll bring her home.``
``Right... understood.@k@vS18/01/160100489. But where...?``
Shion thought for a moment, then smiled brightly.
``...Leave it to me.@k@vS18/06/160600355. I have a plan, but... I'll explain later.@k@vS18/06/160600356. Trust me.``
``...Are you sure?``
``Of course.``
I hoisted Satoko's unconscious body -- heavy as a corpse -- onto my back, carried her to Shion's scooter, and set her on the rear seat.
I leaned her against Shion's back in the driver's seat, wrapped Satoko's arms around Shion's waist, and secured them both with a belt.
That way, Satoko wouldn't slide off.
``All right then, Kei-chan.@k@vS18/06/160600360. I'll leave the rest to you.``
Shion's scooter, with Satoko aboard, glided smoothly down the bumpy road.
In the blink of an eye, the two of them were out of sight.
``Well, let's go.``
After seeing Shion off, Rena started walking toward the main road.
``Go where...?!``
``The festival.@k@vS18/02/160200343. Without an alibi, people will get suspicious...@k@vS18/02/160200344. Remember?``
changeReason: Old says 'To the festival. We need to create an alibi to avoid suspicion... That was part of the plan, wasn't it?'; new says 'The festival. Without an alibi, people will get suspicious... Remember?' Both convey alibi necessity but old explicitly says 'part of the plan,' new is less explicit

TextENGNew: Nearly every villager attended the festival. Just as Rena said, not showing up would raise suspicions.
Honestly, every part of me wanted nothing more than to collapse right here and sleep...
But I dragged my leaden, stick-like legs forward and followed Rena.
``Come on, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS18/02/160200346. Cheer up!@k@vS18/02/160200347. If you look that worn out, people will get suspicious.``
Rena flashed me a smile.
...How could she smile like that?
Suspicion tried to rear its head, but I immediately drove the dark emotion out and berated myself.
Rena is just doing the best she can...! Right...? So I have to do the same...!
...It's all for Satoko!
...To take back our happy, everyday life!
The plan is still underway, right...?
``Rena, lend me your shoulder.``
I braced myself on Rena's shoulder and dragged my heavy legs toward the shrine.
``Keiichi-kun, your arm is surprisingly thick, isn't it? Hau~``
Rena had to be just as exhausted, yet she was cracking jokes. ...Unless that wasn't actually a joke...?!
Walking side by side with Rena, who was faintly blushing, we made our escape from the gloomy forest.
All that was left was to wash away the mud, the rain, the sweat... and the 'impurity' that clung to every inch of us...
And so we headed to Rena's house to change our clothes.
The festivities were in full swing -- the shrine grounds were packed with people.
``Kei-chan! Rena!@k@vS18/03/160300123. You're so late! Where have you two been?!``
Mion glared at Rena and me with the look of someone who had been waiting far too long.
``Sorry about that, Mii-chan~~@k@vS18/02/160200350. Rena forgot something...!``
Rena stuck out her tongue playfully. Seeing that, I immediately followed her lead.
``Seriously~@k@vS18/01/160100494. Girls take way too long with their makeup~@k@vS18/01/160100495. No matter how much you put on, it's not gonna change anything~``
``C-come on~!@k@vS18/02/160200352. That's mean, Keiichi-kun... hau~``
Rena looked at me with a pouty, hurt face.
...Seeing that expression -- so indistinguishable from her usual self -- I nearly choked.
Rena... are you really all right? How can you act so perfectly normal right after committing murder...?
``Keiichi-kun, you're terrible~!``
Rena raised her voice further, prompting me to respond. I killed the serious look on my face and forced on the brightest smile I could manage.
``I'm kidding, I'm kidding.@k@vS18/01/160100497. You look really cute today, Rena!``
``C-cute...? Hau.``
Rena turned red on the spot. Wait, had I actually cheered her up for real...?!
``All right! I'm fired up!@k@vS18/01/160100499. I'll help out with the bargain sales or whatever -- bring it on~``
``Save the jokes for after you buy something!``
The takoyaki vendor laughed good-naturedly.
I laughed back, and when I happened to turn around... my eyes met Mion's, and the look of pained concern on her face stopped me cold.
``...H-hey, um... Kei-chan, Rena.``
``What's up?``
``Well... it's about Satoko...``
Mion trailed off, struggling to get the words out.
She didn't need to say it. I already knew. ...Satoko had ended up leaving the festival early.
Seeing how guilty Mion looked, I gave her a gentle pat on the shoulder.
``...It's not your fault, Mion.``
``That's right.@k@vS18/02/160200356. ...Let's think together tomorrow about how to get Satoko-chan to come back to us.@k@vS18/02/160200357. Okay?``
``Kei-chan... Rena...``
Mion's expression softened, as though a weight had been lifted.
Had she been bracing herself to be blamed for not stopping Satoko?
Don't worry, Mion. We've taken it all back.
The time and the place where Satoko can live with a smile...
changeReason: Old says 'Satoko will be able to smile anytime, anywhere'; new says 'The time and the place where Satoko can live with a smile.' Old promises universal happiness, new is more constrained/specific

TextENGNew: ``Kei-chan, we need to get moving.@k@vS18/03/160300128. Rika-chan's dance performance is about to start.@k@vS18/03/160300129. Let's go watch!``
``Yeah!``
Mion took the lead and guided us forward.
We pushed our worn-out bodies as hard as we could, putting on a show of high spirits.
On the night of Watanagashi, we had unquestionably been at the shrine.
We had been more visibly, boisterously cheerful than anyone else.
And to create that fact, Rena and I had driven our bodies -- battered and spent like worn-out rags -- to their absolute limit...
